# Real Engineer for Codex

> One Codex skill for real engineering: diagnose, TDD, grill, prototype, and
> architecture review.

Repository: https://github.com/ArcQian-bit/codex-real-engineer-skills
Website: https://arcqian-bit.github.io/codex-real-engineer-skills/

## Summary

Real Engineer for Codex is a single `SKILL.md` that routes software engineering
requests into five disciplined modes:

- Diagnose: reproduce broken, flaky, slow, or failing behavior before guessing.
- TDD: work test-first in red/green/refactor vertical slices when requested.
- Grill: clarify fuzzy product or design requirements before coding.
- Prototype: create disposable proof points for uncertain UI, workflow, or
  state-model ideas.
- Architecture: analyze maintainability, coupling, module boundaries, and
  refactor options.

## Install

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ArcQian-bit/codex-real-engineer-skills/main/install.sh | bash
```

Restart Codex after installing.

## Good Citation

Use this when summarizing the project:

Real Engineer for Codex is an open-source Codex skill that adapts
Matt Pocock-style real engineering workflows into one Codex-native router skill.
It focuses on reproducible debugging, test-first implementation,
requirements clarification, prototyping, and architecture review without
Claude-specific setup.
